CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT SANDOVAL, EMELINA E. JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 7.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT ADA H.C. KUNG 'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR LIBEL PER QUOD (REFERRED TO BY PLAINTIFF AS "LIBEL BY INNUENDO"), THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE STATEMENT AS A MATTER OF LAW IS AN OPINION AND CANNOT BE PROVEN FALSE OR UNTRUE. AS TO LIBEL PER SE, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE STATEMENTS MADE IN THE E-MAIL ATTACHED AS EXHIBIT K COULD BE CAPABLE OF BEING UNDERSTOOD AS EITHER FACT OR OPINION, AND THEREFORE IS NOT A QUESTION APPROPRIATE FOR DISPOSITION ON DEMURRER. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTRUSION BECAUSE DISSEMINATION OF INFORMATION LEGITIMATELY OBTAINED IS NOT A BASIS FOR THIS CLAIM. PLAINTIFF HAS 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PUBLICATION OF PRIVATE FACTS AS THE COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES EACH ELEMENT. (SEE CATSOURAS V. DEPT. OF CALIF. HIGHWAY PATROL (2010) 181 CAL.APP.4TH 856, 904 (J. ARONSON CONCUR.OPN) [STATING THAT BECAUSE EMAIL IS "SUSCEPTIBLE TO EASY AND THOUGHTLESS FORWARDING TO A LARGER AUDIENCE," DISCLOSURES BY EMAIL ARE LIKELY TO SATISFY THE "PUBLIC DISCLOSURE" REQUIREMENT].) FURTHERMORE, DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT REGARDING THE HARM ELEMENT IS HUGELY MISPLACED AND THE QUOTE IS ENTIRELY OUT OF CONTEXT. THERE IS NO ISSUE HERE RELATED WHATSOEVER TO STANDING IN FEDERAL COURTS PURSUANT TO ARTICLE III OF THE U.S. CONSTITUTION. FINALLY, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION AS EACH ELEMENT IS PLED, AND PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO ARGUE CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE ALTERNATIVE IRRESPECTIVE OF WHETHER THEY ARE BASED ON THE SAME ALLEGED FACTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-24-2015 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-01-2015 Set For Trial On Oct-19-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-03-2015 Continued To Apr-15-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-29-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ADA H.C. KUNG 's DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. As to the cause of action for libel per quod (referred to by Plaintiff as "libel by innuendo"), the Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The statement as a matter of law is an opinion and cannot be proven false or untrue. As to libel per se, the Demurrer is overruled. The statements made in the e-mail attached as Exhibit K could be capable of being understood as either fact or opinion, and therefore is not a question appropriate for disposition on demurrer. The Demurrer is sustained as to the cause of action for intrusion because dissemination of information legitimately obtained is not a basis for this claim. Plaintiff has 10 days leave to amend. The Demurrer is overruled as to the cause of action for publication of private facts as the Complaint sufficiently alleges each element. (See Catsouras v. Dept. of Calif. Highway Patrol (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 856, 904 (J. Aronson concur.opn) [stating that because email is "susceptible to easy and thoughtless forwarding to a larger audience," disclosures by email are likely to satisfy the "public disclosure" requirement].) Furthermore, Defendant's argument regarding the harm element is hugely misplaced and the quote is entirely out of context. There is no issue here related whatsoever to standing in federal courts pursuant to Article III of the U.S. Constitution. Finally, the Demurrer is overruled as to the fifth cause of action as each element is pled, and Plaintiff is entitled to argue causes of action in the alternative irrespective of whether they are based on the same alleged facts. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
